behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches the interface in the natural-language spec.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "generated function signature matches the expected interface ... specified in the description."
Slot B: Boundary signal: correctness depends on what the live LLM infers from NL and emits as code.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Integration with stubs can only verify parsing/validation, not real model interpretation mistakes.
Rejection gate: Rejected Workflow because behavior text targets a single generation output, not a multi-step agent chain.
Final label: Agentic, driven by Slot B model-judgment dependence and Slot C showing lower-level stubs hide real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real LLM on interface-focused prompts; compile/type-check and assert exact parameter names/types/return type match expected signatures.

behaviors.description: Attempts execute in order and stop immediately on first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt."
Slot B: Boundary signal: orchestrator, test-runner adapter, and attempt state must hand off status correctly.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Unit tests of loop logic miss module handoff bugs (e.g., stale pass/fail propagation).
Integration brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; (b) no dependence on model output quality; (c) no required multi-agentic chain; (d) this is orchestration, but can be validated with deterministic stage outputs.
Rejection gate: Rejected Agentic because behavior text is ordering/early-exit control flow, not quality of LLM decisions.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is cross-module control handoff and Slot C shows Unit is too low.
behaviors.test_description: Wire real orchestrator + attempt bookkeeping + test-result adapter with deterministic stub generation outcomes (fail, fail, pass); assert call order and immediate stop at first pass.

behaviors.description: Live iterative loop can recover from likely first-attempt failure within allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "single call usually fails" and "live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts."
Slot B: Boundary signal: requires sequential generate->test->refine->retest with real LLM outputs affecting later steps.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Single-step Agentic tests cannot catch cross-attempt recovery dynamics and handoff drift.
Final label: Workflow, because Slot B is explicitly multi-step agentic sequencing and Slot C shows Agentic-alone is insufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run full live loop against a target prompt known to commonly fail first; require initial fail then eventual pass within <=3 attempts, validating each transition uses prior failure context.

behaviors.description: Refinement prompt includes failed test names and failure messages after a failed attempt.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt."
Slot B: Boundary signal: failure artifacts from test execution must be transformed and handed to prompt-construction module.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Unit-testing formatter alone misses schema/key mismatches between runner output and prompt builder input.
Integration brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess; (b) correctness is prompt payload wiring, not model judgment; (c) no multi-agentic chain needed; (d) handoff is across stages but can be validated with deterministic failed-test fixtures.
Rejection gate: Rejected Agentic because behavior text checks inclusion of specific fields, not whether model uses them well.
Final label: Integration, based on Slot B cross-module data handoff and Slot C proving Unit is too low.
behaviors.test_description: Use real test-result objects from runner adapter and real prompt builder; assert next prompt contains exact failed test identifiers and failure messages.

behaviors.description: Next live attempt changes implementation in response to prior failure context.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "next live attempt changes implementation content ... rather than resubmitting the same code."
Slot B: Boundary signal: depends on real model behavior when given failure context.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Stubbed responses can be forced to differ and falsely pass even if real model often repeats code.
Rejection gate: Rejected Workflow because behavior text focuses on one model decision for the next attempt’s content change, not full-loop success criteria.
Final label: Agentic, since Slot B requires real model response characteristics and Slot C shows Integration can mask failure.
behaviors.test_description: Provide authentic prior-failure context to a live refinement call; compare new code to previous attempt (structural diff + key-fix evidence) to verify substantive change, not trivial reformat.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns structured error containing all attempts and results.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results."
Slot B: Boundary signal: retry controller, attempt store, and error serializer must coordinate state across iterations.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Unit checks of retry counter miss serialization/aggregation mismatches across modules.
Integration brake: (a) behavior does not require real OS/network/subprocess; (b) does not depend on model output quality; (c) no multi-agentic sequence requirement beyond deterministic fail fixtures; (d) handoff is orchestration/state carryover and is testable with deterministic components.
Rejection gate: Rejected Workflow because behavior text is terminal failure packaging/limit enforcement, not live model decision quality.
Final label: Integration, because Slot B is module-level state aggregation and Slot C excludes Unit as sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestrator with deterministic always-fail generation/test outcomes; assert exactly 3 attempts executed and returned error schema includes each attempt’s code + test result payload.

behaviors.description: Deterministic non-agentic end-to-end wiring works with stub model outputs.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "full non-agentic pipeline wiring ... works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs."
Slot B: Boundary signal: explicit cross-module wiring check (generate call, test execution, bookkeeping) without live model dependence.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Unit tests cannot expose interface mismatches across pipeline stages.
Integration brake: (a) behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; (b) explicitly "non-agentic" and "stubbed" disqualifies model-quality dependence; (c) no required multi-agentic chain; (d) orchestration is present but intentionally deterministic.
Rejection gate: Rejected System because no concrete requirement for real subprocess/network; rejected Agentic due explicit non-agentic stubs.
Final label: Integration, per Slot B module wiring boundary and Slot C lower-level blind spots.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full pipeline in-process with fixed stub outputs across attempts; validate generated code is passed to test executor and bookkeeping captures statuses consistently end to end.

behaviors.description: Full live loop solves at least one known hard function that fails on first attempt.
behaviors.justification:
Slot A: Evidence quote: "full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt."
Slot B: Boundary signal: requires multiple real model decisions plus cross-step state carryover to achieve eventual pass.
Slot C: Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Agentic single-call tests cannot validate iterative recovery through chained decisions.
Final label: Workflow, because Slot B is explicitly multi-step live agent pipeline and Slot C rules out single-step Agentic coverage.
behaviors.test_description: Run live end-to-end loop on a curated hard prompt where baseline first attempt fails; assert first-attempt fail is observed, refinement occurs, and a later attempt passes within attempt budget.
